title: Surveying and Mapping (except Geophysical) Services

alternatives.name: Land-development, cadastral, and construction surveying firms
inclusion_criteria: The firm is classified in NAICS 541370 and is in the frozen $1-10M EBITDA target band.
Boundary, property-line, and cadastral surveying, subdivision layout, plus construction surveying is the largest of the four enumerated annual service-revenue families.
The firm provides field measurement, monument and boundary work, construction layout, plats, legal descriptions, and related office processing under applicable professional-surveying responsibility.
A tie in largest annual service-revenue family is assigned to the lexicographically smallest archetype ID.
exclusion_criteria: Topographic, planimetric, and geodetic surveying is the largest annual service-revenue family.
Geospatial image acquisition, processing, interpretation, or geospatial consulting is the largest annual service-revenue family.
Hydrographic and bathymetric surveying is the largest annual service-revenue family.
Geophysical surveying, engineering, architectural services, or map publishing is the firm's primary activity.
alternatives.rationale: The 2022 Economic Census assigns 27.4% of 541370 revenue to boundary/property-line/cadastral work, 7.7% to subdivision layout and design, and 19.9% to construction surveying. Applying the combined 55.0% revenue share to the frozen 119-firm band gives 65.45, rounded to 65. Revenue share is a proxy for the share of firms led by this service family; the range allows for mixed-service firms, unequal firm revenue, target-band mix differences, and the dataset's LOW-quality band estimate.

alternatives.name: Topographic, planimetric, and geodetic surveying firms
inclusion_criteria: The firm is classified in NAICS 541370 and is in the frozen $1-10M EBITDA target band.
Topographic and planimetric surveying and mapping plus geodetic and ground-control work is the largest of the four enumerated annual service-revenue families.
A tie in largest annual service-revenue family is assigned to the lexicographically smallest archetype ID.
exclusion_criteria: Land-development, cadastral, subdivision, and construction surveying is the largest annual service-revenue family.
Geospatial image acquisition, processing, interpretation, or consulting is the largest annual service-revenue family.
Hydrographic and bathymetric surveying is the largest annual service-revenue family.
Geophysical data acquisition or interpretation is the firm's primary activity.
alternatives.rationale: The 2022 Economic Census reports 21.6% of 541370 revenue from topographic and planimetric surveying and mapping and 1.6% from geodetic surveying and ground-control support. Applying 23.2% to 119 gives 27.61, rounded to 28. The range reflects service overlap, sampling error, unequal firm revenue, and possible target-band mix differences.

alternatives.name: Geospatial imagery, data-processing, and consulting firms
inclusion_criteria: The firm is classified in NAICS 541370 and is in the frozen $1-10M EBITDA target band.
Geospatial photo or image acquisition, image processing, geospatial data interpretation, and geospatial consulting is the largest of the four enumerated annual service-revenue families.
The firm remains a surveying or mapping service provider rather than a software publisher, data host, or geophysical surveyor.
A tie in largest annual service-revenue family is assigned to the lexicographically smallest archetype ID.
exclusion_criteria: Land-development, cadastral, subdivision, and construction surveying is the largest annual service-revenue family.
Topographic, planimetric, and geodetic surveying is the largest annual service-revenue family.
Hydrographic and bathymetric surveying is the largest annual service-revenue family.
Software development, database publishing, aerial transportation, or geophysical surveying is the firm's primary activity.
alternatives.rationale: The 2022 Economic Census reports 4.3% of 541370 revenue from geospatial image acquisition, 2.9% from image processing, 3.6% from geospatial data interpretation, and 1.9% from geospatial consulting. Applying the combined 12.7% share to 119 gives 15.11, rounded to 15. The range also accommodates suppressed thematic-mapping revenue and the possibility that capital-intensive geospatial firms contribute more revenue per firm than land-survey firms.

alternatives.name: Hydrographic and bathymetric surveying firms
inclusion_criteria: The firm is classified in NAICS 541370 and is in the frozen $1-10M EBITDA target band.
Hydrographic and bathymetric surveying and mapping is the largest of the four enumerated annual service-revenue families.
A tie in largest annual service-revenue family is assigned to the lexicographically smallest archetype ID.
exclusion_criteria: Land-development, cadastral, subdivision, and construction surveying is the largest annual service-revenue family.
Topographic, planimetric, and geodetic surveying is the largest annual service-revenue family.
Geospatial image acquisition, processing, interpretation, or consulting is the largest annual service-revenue family.
Marine geophysical data acquisition or interpretation is the firm's primary activity.
alternatives.rationale: The 2022 Economic Census reports 2.4% of 541370 revenue from hydrographic and bathymetric surveying and mapping. Applying 2.4% to 119 gives 2.86, rounded to 3. The wide range reflects the small reported line, high service overlap, unequal revenue per firm, and boundary risk with geophysical work.

residual.name: Other, cross-over, or unclassifiable in-band surveying and mapping firms
residual.rationale: The four enumerated revenue families allocate 111 of the frozen 119 band firms. The residual retains eight firms for thematic or cartographic mapping with suppressed revenue, other products, atypical mixes, and firms whose largest service family cannot be assigned from aggregate product-line data.

sources.title: 2022 Economic Census: Industry by Products Statistics for the U.S. (ECNNAPCSIND2022)
NAICS definition, illustrative examples, and cross-references for 541370 Surveying and Mapping (except Geophysical) Services
Census Data API variables for 2022 Industry by Products Statistics

target_archetype.selection_basis: The frozen dataset estimates 119 firms in the target band. Aggregate 2022 Census product-line revenue shares are mapped to mutually exclusive largest-annual-revenue-family archetypes: land-development/cadastral/construction, 55.0% x 119 = 65; topographic/planimetric/geodetic, 23.2% x 119 = 28; geospatial imagery/data/consulting, 12.7% x 119 = 15; and hydrographic/bathymetric, 2.4% x 119 = 3. The residual is 119 - 111 = 8. The land-development/cadastral archetype has the largest base count. Its low count of 50 remains above the next-largest high count of 42, so the supported ranges do not overlap at the top. Counts are estimates rather than direct firm classifications.

value_basis.scope: Employee and controllable-contractor cash cost used to deliver the selected in-band land-development, cadastral, and construction surveying firm's controlled value added; excludes documented third-party pass-through deducted from recognized revenue and excludes owner distributions, interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization.
value_basis.controllable_value_added_boundary: Recognized revenue minus only documented consideration contractually or economically owed to an outside provider that controls its separately scoped performance and whose work is not directed, integrated, quality-controlled, or accepted under the surveying firm's professional responsibility. Subcontracted field surveying, CAD, geomatics, data processing, or technical production directed or integrated by the firm is controllable-contractor cash cost, not pass-through. A third-party cost deducted from revenue may not also enter employee or controllable-contractor cash cost; any retained coordination fee or markup remains controllable value added.
approved_pass_through_categories: Government recording, filing, and permit fees billed at documented third-party cost
Purchased public records, title documents, imagery, or external datasets billed at documented third-party cost

Separately scoped aviation, vessel, utility-location, or specialist subconsultant services when the outside provider controls performance and the surveying firm retains only a separately identified coordination fee or markup

roles.title: Licensed professional surveyors and technical project leadership
roles.rationale: Assumed cash-cost share for responsible-charge review, boundary judgment, project scoping, client counsel, supervision, signing or sealing, and technical accountability.

roles.title: Field survey crews and field supervision
roles.rationale: Assumed cash-cost share for crew chiefs, instrument operators, survey technicians, site mobilization, measurement, monument recovery or setting, and field quality control.

roles.title: CAD, geomatics, drafting, and office production
roles.rationale: Assumed cash-cost share for data reduction, CAD drafting, surface and model production, plats, legal-description support, records integration, and deliverable preparation.

roles.title: Project, client, permitting, and records coordination
roles.rationale: Assumed cash-cost share for scheduling, budgets, client communication, utility or public-record coordination, deliverable routing, and billing support.

roles.title: Business development, administration, and nontechnical operations
roles.rationale: Assumed cash-cost share for sales, finance, people operations, office administration, fleet coordination, and leadership not included in technical project work.

roles.title: Controllable specialist and overflow contractors
roles.rationale: Assumed cash-cost share for outside field crews, drafting, scanning, imagery, utility-location, or specialist production whose work the operator directs, integrates, quality-controls, or accepts under its professional responsibility.

value_basis.rationale: The normalized dataset's labor share is measured at six-digit NAICS 541370, but it does not separate the selected service family, employee cash cost from controllable contractors, or recognized revenue from controllable value-added revenue. Its occupational mix is at four-digit 5413 and is dominated by engineering and architectural occupations, so it cannot support target-specific surveying weights. The specification therefore freezes a transparent class-neutral assumption: six mutually exclusive cash-cost roles sum to 1.00, use ESTIMATE/NONE with no sources, and distinguish controlled subcontract production from genuine third-party pass-through.
caveats: Role weights are shares of employee plus controllable-contractor cash cost, not headcount, billed hours, recognized revenue, task time, or a claim about any role's future treatment.
Owner labor belongs in employee cash cost at market replacement compensation even when historical accounts classify part of it as distributions or transaction add-backs.
An outside provider directed by the firm and integrated into its deliverable is a controllable contractor, not denominator pass-through merely because the client reimburses the invoice.
Approved categories are boundary labels, not automatic deductions; contract and accounting evidence must document the outside-provider amount for every deduction.
Do not substitute the broader 5413 occupational mix for, or reweight, this frozen assumption.

source_hints.source: Target-company contracts, general ledger, project billing detail, revenue-recognition policy, subconsultant invoices, reimbursable-expense schedules, and state DOT or federal architect-engineer audit guidance
source_hints.why: Reconcile recognized revenue to billed amounts and document each outside-provider category before deducting it from controllable value-added revenue.

source_hints.source: Target payroll and benefits ledger, time sheets, contractor ledger, project-cost reports, National Society of Professional Surveyors benchmarks, and surveying-specific compensation or operations surveys
source_hints.why: Replace broad-industry inference with target-archetype employee cost, controlled contractor cost, and cash-cost role weights while preserving the frozen role partition.

source_hints.source: State surveying statutes and board rules, NSPS model standards, ALTA/NSPS standards, public procurement scopes, standard operating procedures, and professional-liability guidance
source_hints.why: Identify required responsible charge, field verification, monument decisions, records research, review, signing or sealing, and retained operator accountability.

source_hints.source: Target contract cohorts, backlog reports, public procurement records, construction and land-development customer data, state and local capital plans, and customer interviews
source_hints.why: Separate repeat project flow, master-service arrangements, public qualifications-based selection, development cyclicality, and client switching from gross end-market demand.

source_hints.source: Closed surveying-firm transactions, state license and ownership records, Axial, DealStats, BizBuySell, AEC transaction advisers, and dated sale processes
source_hints.why: Count control transactions meeting the service-family and size boundary, then distinguish firms a reference buyer could legally own, access, diligence, and win.

source_hints.source: Closed AEC and surveying transactions, quality-of-earnings reports, lender underwriting, ownership-transition surveys, license rosters, and post-transaction management histories
source_hints.why: Normalize earnings and test paid multiples, management depth, responsible-charge continuity, key-client transfer, and licensed succession for the selected archetype.

controllable_value_added: What recognized-revenue policy applies to fixed-fee, hourly, unit-price, reimbursable, and subcontracted surveying work, and can annual recognized revenue be reconciled to the general ledger and project billing records?

For travel, recording fees, purchased imagery or records, equipment or vehicle rental, aviation or vessel services, utility-location services, and subconsultants, what base/low/high amount is contractually or economically owed to an outside provider rather than retained as controlled value added?

Which outside providers perform separately specified pass-through work and which work under the firm's direction, professional responsibility, integration, and quality control as controllable contractors?
Does recognized revenue minus documented pass-through remain positive and bounded in every supported case without deducting any cost again from the cash-cost numerator?

What are employee cash compensation, associated cash benefits and burden, and controllable-contractor cash cost for the selected archetype on the same period and accounting basis as controllable value-added revenue?
How should market replacement compensation for owner labor be separated from distributions, excess compensation, and transaction add-backs?

What target evidence supports cash-cost weights for licensed technical leadership, field crews, CAD and geomatics production, project coordination, business operations, and controlled specialists, with weights summing to one, and which inputs remain missing if such evidence does not exist?
operational_realization: Which field, records-research, data-reduction, drafting, checking, project-control, and deliverable steps can be standardized or delegated, and which remain under licensed responsible charge or require site-specific judgment?

What implementation schedule, training, data conversion, system integration, field-equipment compatibility, quality assurance, cybersecurity, and change-management costs are supported for 100-499 employee land-surveying firms?
How do rework, boundary disputes, field revisit rates, professional-liability exposure, and signing or sealing requirements constrain achievable workflow changes?
commercial_retention: What share of revenue arises from repeat developers, contractors, utilities, municipalities, master-service agreements, qualifications-based selections, and one-off property transactions?
How do fixed-fee, hourly, unit-price, cost-plus, and reimbursable terms transmit lower delivery cost through renegotiation, rebid, rate-card review, or competitive tender?

Are clients purchasing measured deliverables, licensed judgment, local records knowledge, field response capacity, named professionals, or bundled project relationships, and what evidence supports each retention mechanism?
sale_availability: How many closed U.S. control sales of independent land-development, cadastral, and construction surveying firms in the target band can be verified over a defined recent five-year window?

What share of the frozen archetype population became genuinely sale-available during that window after removing minority recapitalizations, internal ownership transfers, and firms outside the size or service boundary?
What evidence supports each endpoint of a five-year sale-availability range, including owner age, succession, professional-entity restrictions, and observed transaction flow?
buyer_access_win: What share of target firms is legally ownable by the reference buyer under state professional-entity, licensed-control, and responsible-charge rules?
Among sale-available firms, what evidence supports the reference buyer's conditional ability to source, access, diligence, and win without an incumbent relationship or privileged channel?
How do geography, local license coverage, public-client approvals, bonding or insurance, seller transition demands, and competing AEC buyers affect access and win probability separately from price?
valuation_robustness: What entry multiples are supported by closed target-archetype transactions after normalizing owner compensation, pass-through revenue, subcontractor treatment, equipment or fleet ownership, working capital, and one-time project effects?
What single year-five operating state is supported for size, service mix, customer concentration, management depth, license coverage, and accounting policy?

Conditional on that same operating state, what downside exit multiple is supported by capital-market, size, and liquidity evidence without adding penalties for demand, pricing, or operating effects represented elsewhere?
operator_survival: What constant-price volume of land-development, cadastral, subdivision, and construction surveying service remains under operator control through year five after separating construction-cycle price effects?

Which licensed professionals, field leaders, project managers, client relationships, local records knowledge, and public qualifications must remain for the acquired operating entity to function after founder transition?

What evidence shows that responsible charge, signing or sealing authority, key-client retention, staff supervision, and new-business origination transfer to successors rather than remain personal to the seller?

special_notes: This record describes land-development, cadastral, subdivision, and construction surveying firms, not all of NAICS 541370.
The frozen n_band estimate of 119 has LOW quality. Census product revenue shares allocate that uncertain denominator and do not directly classify firms by their largest service family.
Census product lines coexist within establishments. The largest-revenue-family rule makes the archetypes mutually exclusive, but the resulting firm counts remain a proxy with broad ranges.

No target-specific pass-through percentage is frozen. Every proposed deduction from recognized revenue requires a separately documented category and bounded amount; otherwise it remains missing and gross billed revenue may not silently replace controllable value-added revenue.

Reimbursed cost is not automatically pass-through. Work directed, integrated, quality-controlled, or accepted under the surveying firm's professional responsibility belongs in controllable contractor cash cost and may not also reduce the denominator.
Only the outside-provider amount is deductible as pass-through. Any retained coordination fee or markup remains part of controllable value-added revenue.
The value-basis role partition is a source-free class-neutral assumption. Preserve its membership and weights, and do not reweight it from the four-digit 5413 occupational mix.

Licensing, responsible-charge, signing or sealing, professional-entity ownership, and local public-procurement requirements vary by state and must be tested for the target geography rather than generalized nationally.
